chunks.text: So, let's continue with the Math section in Stryver's DSA course. But before that, hey, welcome back to the channel. I hope you guys are doing extremely well. So, the problem that we will be solving today is check if a given number is prime or not. So, what is the definition of a prime number? Will you say that the definition of a prime number is a number which is divisible by 1 and itself? Is it a correct definition? Probably not. This is not a correct definition because for this, if I take the number n equal to 1, then this definition will hold because the number is divisible by 1 and itself and itself is 1, it's here, right? So, this is a prime number which is not the case. So, a better definition is a number which is divisible by 1 and itself and the total number of divisors and the total number of divisors is 2. 1 and itself. No one else. Got it?
